heading: Innovation Fund
label: 10007 Innovation Fund
notes: References in Text, referred to in subsec. (b)(1)(A), was amended generally by , , , and, as so amended, subsec. (b)(2) does not describe achievement gaps.Section 6311 of this titlePub. L. 114–95, title I, § 1005129 Stat. 1820Dec. 10, 2015

Amendments2009—Subsec. (a)(3). , amended par. (3) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The Secretary shall make awards to eligible entities that have made significant gains in closing the achievement gap as described in subsection (b)(1)—Pub. L. 111–117, § 307(1)“(A) to allow such eligible entities to expand their work and serve as models for best practices;“(B) to allow such eligible entities to work in partnership with the private sector and the philanthropic community; and“(C) to identify and document best practices that can be shared, and taken to scale based on demonstrated success.”Subsec. (b). , redesignated pars. (1), (2), (3), and (4) as (1)(A), (1)(B), (2), and (3), respectively, inserted “or” after semicolon in par. (1)(A), amended par. (1)(B) generally, and substituted “it has established one or more partnerships” for “they have established partnerships” in par. (3). Prior to amendment, par. (1)(B) read as follows: “have exceeded the State’s annual measurable objectives consistent with such for 2 or more consecutive years or have demonstrated success in significantly increasing student academic achievement for all groups of students described in such section through another measure, such as measures described in ;”.Pub. L. 111–117, § 307(2)section 6311(b)(2) of this titlesection 6311(c)(2) of this titleSubsec. (c). , substituted “paragraphs (1)(A) or (1)(B) and (2) of subsection (b) if the nonprofit organization has a record of significantly improving student achievement, attainment, or retention and shall be considered to have met the requirements of subsection (b)(3) if it demonstrates that it will meet the requirement relating to private-sector matching” for “paragraphs (1), (2), (3) of subsection (b) if such nonprofit organization has a record of meeting such requirements”.Pub. L. 111–117, § 307(3)Subsec. (d). , added subsec. (d).Pub. L. 111–117, § 307(4)

children.label: i
children.text: one or more local educational agencies; or

children.heading: Program established
children.label: 2 Program established
children.text: From the total amount reserved under , the Secretary may reserve up to $650,000,000 to establish an Innovation Fund, which shall consist of academic achievement awards that recognize eligible entities that meet the requirements described in subsection (b).section 10001(c) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: expand their work and serve as models for best practices; and

children.label: B
children.text: work in partnership with the private sector and the philanthropic community.

children.label: A
children.text: have significantly closed the achievement gaps between groups of students described in section 6311(b)(2) of this title; or1See References in Text note below.1

children.label: B
children.text: have demonstrated success in significantly increasing student academic achievement for all groups of students described in such section;

have made significant improvement in other areas, such as graduation rates or increased recruitment and placement of high-quality teachers and school leaders, as demonstrated with meaningful data; and

demonstrate that it has established one or more partnerships with the private sector, which may include philanthropic organizations, and that the private sector will provide matching funds in order to help bring results to scale.

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: c Special rule
children.text: In the case of an eligible entity that includes a nonprofit organization, the eligible entity shall be considered to have met the eligibility requirements of paragraphs (1)(A) or (1)(B) and (2) of subsection (b) if the nonprofit organization has a record of significantly improving student achievement, attainment, or retention and shall be considered to have met the requirements of subsection (b)(3) if it demonstrates that it will meet the requirement relating to private-sector matching.

children.heading: Subgrants
children.label: d Subgrants
children.text: In the case of an eligible entity that is a partnership described in subsection (a)(1)(B), the partner serving as the fiscal agent may make subgrants to one or more of the other entities in the partnership.
